'''Brad Walker''' (born 30 January 1998) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]] and [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and spent time on loan from [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] at the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] and also spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

==Background==
He was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], [[Cumbria]], England.

==Playing career==
===Widnes Vikings===
On 9 Jun 2016, he made his [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] [[Super League]] début coming off the bench in the Round 18 28-28 victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Rugby League Project|date=9 Jun 2016|title=Castleford Tigers 28 lost to Widnes Vikings 38|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xxi-2016/round-18/castleford-vs-widnes/summary.html}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 8 December 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Dec 2023|title=Championship club snap up former Super League young gun with utility making move to hometown club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-club-snap-up-former-super-league-young-gun-with-utility-making-move-to-hometown-club}}</ref>
